agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical SQL injection vulnerability — user-controlled inputs are interpolated directly into raw SQL with no parameterization or sanitization."
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via unsanitized f-string query construction",
      "risk": "An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter, enabling full database read/write/delete, authentication bypass, and potential remote code execution depending on DB engine.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (query construction)",
      "reproduction": "Call the public API with customer_id set to: ' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --  This produces: SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --' AND status = '...'",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter))",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security, data integrity, availability — all compromised"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public-facing parameters",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, customer_id and status_filter have no type, length, or allowlist validation. customer_id could be empty/null causing unexpected results; status_filter should be constrained to known enum values.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries function signature and body",
      "reproduction": "Call with status_filter='' or customer_id=None — returns unexpected result sets or errors depending on DB driver behavior with None-to-string coercion.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id is non-empty and matches expected format (e.g., UUID regex). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses (e.g., {'pending', 'in_transit', 'delivered', 'cancelled'}). Reject invalid inputs before query execution.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input validation, defense in depth"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded columns and rows",
      "risk": "SELECT * exposes every column (potentially sensitive internal fields like driver_notes, internal_cost, GPS coordinates) to the API caller. No LIMIT clause means a broad filter can dump the entire table, causing data leakage and potential OOM/timeout under load.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries function, query string",
      "reproduction": "A valid customer with thousands of deliveries triggers a full table scan with no pagination. Combined with the injection bug, an attacker can SELECT * from any table.",
      "fix": "Explicitly enumerate safe columns (e.g., SELECT id, status, estimated_delivery, ...) and add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination with a sane default (e.g., LIMIT 50).",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data minimization, performance, API reliability"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No authorization check — any customer can query any other customer's deliveries",
      "description": "customer_id comes directly from query params with no verification that the authenticated user owns that customer_id. An attacker can enumerate other customers' delivery data by iterating IDs.",
      "location": "search_deliveries caller (API layer)"
    },
    {
      "title": "No error handling around db_conn.execute",
      "description": "Database errors (connection lost, timeout, malformed query) will propagate as unhandled exceptions, likely leaking stack traces and internal schema details to the API caller.",
      "location": "search_deliveries function"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Add structured logging for query execution to support audit trails on this public-facing endpoint.",
    "Consider an ORM or query builder instead of raw SQL to prevent future injection regressions.",
    "Add rate limiting at the API layer to prevent enumeration and abuse of the search endpoint."
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is actively exploitable the moment it reaches any environment accessible to untrusted input. It should not be deployed.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this should not ship in any form.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing to parameterized queries is a transparent change with no API-level breaking impact.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics — exploitation would be invisible.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["auth_security: endpoint lacks authorization and is vulnerable to SQL injection, exposing all data in the database"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy. If already deployed, take the endpoint offline immediately and rotate database credentials as a precaution."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 99,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "SQL injection is textbook-certain — f-string interpolation of user input into SQL with no escaping.",
      "Missing input validation is directly observable.",
      "SELECT * over-exposure and missing LIMIT are directly observable."
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Authorization logic may exist in a middleware layer not shown — flagged as important issue rather than critical."
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "customer_id and status_filter reach this function unsanitized from HTTP query params, as stated in the prompt.",
      "db_conn.execute does not auto-parameterize f-strings (no known Python DB driver does)."
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking: SQL injection vulnerability, clear as day.
